{"key_event":"Thatcher centralization and miners’ strike defeat","reasoning":"The central state regained capacity after the 1970s through executive centralization, legal constraints on unions, policing powers, and the defeat of the miners, though privatization reduced direct state ownership of production. Civil society remained very strong by comparative standards—free press, courts, parties, associations, and emerging protest networks—but organized labour, local government, and working-class civic institutions lost substantial leverage.","society_change":-0.7,"society_power":9.2,"state_change":0.5,"state_power":9.3}
